hook.text: There are over 400 'dead zones' in the world's oceans where almost nothing can survive.

buildup.text: Fertilizer runoff from farms feeds algae blooms near coasts. The algae grow out of control.

discovery.text: When the algae die, bacteria decompose them and consume all the dissolved oxygen.

twist.text: Fish, crabs, and shrimp suffocate or flee. What's left is a barren seabed.

climax.text: The Gulf of Mexico dead zone covers over 15,000 square kilometers every summer.

punchline.text: Too much fertilizer on land kills life at sea.

quiz.question: What causes ocean dead zones?
options: Fertilizer runoff triggers algae blooms that consume oxygen
